summary: Validated the redesigned PIX payment modal on /minhas-inscricoes.html end-to-end (backend + frontend). All 6 acceptance criteria PASSED. Backend endpoints /api/pix-config and /api/pix/generate return the registered key 15537652730 / INSCRIÇÃO ENARE / BRASILIA DF and a valid BR Code + QR PNG base64. Frontend modal renders the QR inside #pixQrWrap, fills #pixCode with the EMV BR Code (starts with 00020101, contains 15537652730), the copy button changes to 'Código copiado!', and the Fechar button removes the modal.

issues: Demo card uses placeholder data: Nome shows 'CANDIDATO' and CPF shows '---'. This is the seeded example inscription; in production with real CPF the values will be populated. Cosmetic only — not blocking.

critical_code_review_comments: Backend /api/pix/generate is public (no auth). Anyone can mint BR Codes with the configured key. Acceptable for this app since the key is intentionally public on the payment page, but worth flagging.

pix_code printed on /minhas-inscricoes.html includes only one inscription valor (R$ 330,00) but the backend curl sample used R$ 220 — backend correctly uses the value posted by the frontend. No issue.

minhas-inscricoes.html builds the modal via inline JS string templating after click; on slow networks the ~700ms delay before fetch could feel sluggish. Consider showing a skeleton inside #pixQrWrap immediately (already implemented as 'Gerando QR Code...'). OK as is.
CSP header on the HTML correctly allows connect-src 'self' and img-src 'self' data: — QR data: URL renders fine.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PIX flow verified on /minhas-inscricoes.html. Test steps: (1) load page → demo card with status 'Pagamento Pendente' renders, (2) click [data-testid=btn-pagar] → modal opens with title 'Pagamento via PIX' and dark-blue header + green badge, (3) wait ~2-3s for POST /api/pix/generate to resolve — #pixQrWrap will contain an (len ~3000), (4) #pixCode is filled with EMV BR Code starting '00020101...' and containing the configured key '15537652730', (5) [data-testid=pix-copiar] text toggles to 'Código copiado!' on click, (6) the 'Fechar' button removes the modal from DOM. Note: browser headless_shell occasionally segfaults on first launch — retry once if the very first run dies.
